May require verifying Durable Object bindings are available in the acceptance vitest project.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T00:02:43Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-16T00:42:49Z"}

{"id":"turtlebased-ts-852.4","title":"Fix US04 rate-limiting acceptance test infrastructure (timeout + DO storage isolation)","description":"The US04 rate-limiting acceptance test body is correctly written but fails due to vitest-pool-workers infrastructure issues. Three fixes needed: (1) Increase timeout on the rate-limiting it() block to ~30s (6+ sign-in attempts × 2 HTTP roundtrips each with DO storage ops exceeds 5000ms default). (2) Add Durable Object state cleanup in beforeEach of setup.ts — currently only D1 tables are cleared, but DO rate-limit counters leak between tests. (3) Evaluate whether isolatedStorage needs to be disabled for the acceptance project's DO bindings to prevent the 'Failed to push isolated storage stack frame' error that cascades when DOs are aborted mid-operation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T00:35:13Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-16T00:35:13Z"}

{"id":"turtlebased-ts-bjd.6.35","title":"[quality] Weak acceptance test assertion in US15 dashboard auth guard","description":"File: generated-acceptance-tests/US15-dashboard-auth-guard.spec.ts

Severity: Medium

expect(res.status).not.toBe(302) passes on 500 or 404, so it does not actually verify the dashboard is accessible to an authenticated user.

{"id":"turtlebased-ts-bjd.6.44","title":"[quality] Missing sign-in status assertion in US13 acceptance test","description":"File: generated-acceptance-tests/US13-client-side-auth-status-check.spec.ts
Line: 41
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Sign-in response status is never asserted before reading Set-Cookie; a failed sign-in would cause the subsequent cookie assertion to pass vacuously.

Fix:
Add expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) before reading the cookie header.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:15Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:15Z"}

{"id":"turtlebased-ts-bjd.6.45","title":"[quality] Missing and weak status assertions in US14 acceptance test","description":"File: generated-acceptance-tests/US14-authenticated-navbar-swap.spec.ts
Lines: 24, 98
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Missing status assertion for sign-in (line 24); expect(res.status).not.toBe(302) (line 98) passes on any non-redirect including 500.

Fix:
Assert expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) at line 24; replace line 98 with expect(res.status).toBe(200).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:15Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:15Z"}

{"id":"turtlebased-ts-bjd.6.48","title":"[quality] Missing status assertion before Location header in US15 spec","description":"File: generated-acceptance-tests/US15-dashboard-auth-guard.spec.ts
Line: 86
Severity: Low

Problem:
expect(signInRes.headers.get('Location')).toBe('/dashboard/') does not first assert signInRes.status === 303, so a stray Location header from a different redirect would pass unnoticed.

Fix:
Add expect(signInRes.status).toBe(303) before the Location header assertion.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"david@worldsenoughstudios.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:17Z","created_by":"eykd","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:09:17Z"}
